title: Crunchy Japanese Chicken Onigiri
description: A solo dinner Japanese dish featuring roasted chicken and crunchy onigiri, made with rice syrup and jocheong, and cured with shio koji, all cooked in a copper pot for a low calorie friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a copper pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast to the copper pot and cook until browned on all sides.
Transfer the chicken to the oven and roast for 20 minutes.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the onigiri by mixing the Japanese rice with rice syrup and jocheong in a bowl.
Add the shio koji to the onigiri mixture and stir well.
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
Shape the onigiri mixture into triangles and pan fry until crispy and golden brown.
Serve the sliced chicken with the crunchy onigiri and enjoy.
